title: Pyrus indurata
intro: Pyrus indurata, also known as the Japanese mountain apple or Asian mountain ash, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus indurata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pyrus indurata is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is also used to make a poultice for topical application.
taxonomy: Pyrus indurata is classified in the plant kingdom as a species of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the rose family. This family also includes other popular fruit trees like apples and pears. Within the Rosaceae family, Pyrus indurata is further classified as a member of the genus Pyrus, which consists of several species of pear trees. The species Pyrus indurata is native to the Himalayas and is also found in parts of China.
